pattern: A state of (position, deletions still allowed) captures every choice, and trying every possible run length from that position keeps the search small without ever materializing the compressed string.
hints: Think of scanning left to right deciding, at each position, whether to delete the current character; the state that matters is just (index, deletions remaining).

From a state, try keeping a run of the current character: scan forward, optionally paying deletions for characters that don't match, and use the encoded-length rule for a run (no digits for a run of 1, one digit for 2-9, two digits for 10-99, and so on).

Compare that against simply deleting the current character and moving on with one less deletion budget; take the minimum over both choices and memoize on the state so repeated states are computed once.

edge_inputs.description: no deletions allowed, plain run-length encode
one deletion removes a lone middle character
deleting to merge two same-letter runs beats deleting individually
run length crosses the single-digit encoding boundary
all distinct characters, deletions only remove letters outright

stress.description: short strings over a two-letter alphabet with a random deletion budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 12))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('ab') for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(0, n)
    return [s, k]
